paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling

raw_extraction.organization: UC Berkeley, Facebook AI Research, Google Brain
raw_extraction.innovation: Decision Transformer casts reinforcement learning as conditional sequence modeling, using a causal Transformer to predict future actions given past states, actions, and desired return-to-go. This avoids bootstrapping and credit assignment challenges inherent in traditional RL methods.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Offline RL datasets (Atari, OpenAI Gym, Key-to-Door)
raw_extraction.application: Offline RL benchmarks (Atari, OpenAI Gym, Key-to-Door), trajectory generation, sparse/reward settings, long-term credit assignment
raw_extraction.research_problem: Offline reinforcement learning and policy optimization without dynamic programming or value pessimism
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Autoregressive sequence modeling of (return-to-go, state, action) tuples
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Offline reinforcement learning via supervised sequence modeling conditioned on target returns
raw_extraction.extension: Uses modality-specific linear embeddings for returns-to-go, states, and actions, combined with learned episode timestep embeddings instead of standard positional embeddings.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling

raw_extraction.innovation: **Key innovation**: Frame offline RL as a **sequence modeling problem**, treating trajectories as sequences of (state, action, return-to-go) tuples and predicting future returns-to-go conditioned on past states/actions. This avoids explicit credit assignment and enables scalable offline RL via standard language-modeling techniques.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Offline datasets (Atari, MuJoCo, custom graph shortest-path task)
raw_extraction.application: Offline RL in robotics (MuJoCo), Atari games, graph shortest-path problems
raw_extraction.license: Closed source (no explicit license mentioned; code available via supplementary materials)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable offline reinforcement learning without exploration or environment interaction
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Offline reinforcement learning via sequence modeling (predicting returns-to-go, state-action pairs)
raw_extraction.context_length: Variable (50 for Atari, 20 for OpenAI Gym)
raw_extraction.extension: Linear decoder head projects encoded (state, action, return-to-go) embeddings into next-token prediction space, enabling joint modeling of returns-to-go and actions.
raw_extraction.paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling

raw_extraction.innovation: Framework for reinforcement learning using a sequence model (Transformer) to predict cumulative rewards (returns) conditioned on state-action histories, eliminating the need for direct policy optimization.
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Training dataset generated via random walks on graph-structured environments (e.g., MuJoCo, AntMaze, Adroit)
raw_extraction.application: Robotics control, autonomous navigation, decision-making in sequential environments
raw_extraction.license: Not explicitly stated (likely open-source based on academic publication)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable reinforcement learning without direct policy gradients or reward signals
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Sequence modeling over (return, state, action) tuples
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Reinforcement Learning via sequence-to-sequence prediction of future returns given state-action trajectories
raw_extraction.paper_title: Decision Transformer: Reinforcement Learning via Sequence Modeling
